purpose: Let the user compare outbound and return flight options side-by-side inside tabbed comparison tables.
primaryUserGoal: Pick a flight itinerary that balances price, stops, and time.
whenHermesShouldChoose: User asks to compare flights, find a round trip, search for the cheapest or most direct option between two airports.
goodFor: Deciding between a cheaper long layover and a pricier direct
Seeing price / duration / stops in one grid
supports: Tabs for outbound and return legs (and optional multi-city)
Emphasized price cells with success tone for best value
idealDataShape: Origin, destination, outbound date, return date, passengers
Per-leg: 3-6 options with carrier, depart, arrive, duration, stops, price

populationInstructions.summary: Create a tabs section with 'Outbound' and 'Return' panes. Each pane contains a comparison-table: one row per itinerary with carrier logo (leadingImage, query: '<carrier> logo'), cells for depart, arrive, duration, stops, price.
steps: Parse origin, destination, outbound date, return date, passengers.
For each leg, produce 4-5 rows sorted by a sensible default (price ascending).
Row label: carrier + flight number. leadingImage query: '<carrier> airline logo'.
Cells: depart (local time + airport), arrive (local time + airport), duration, stops, price.
Mark the best-value itinerary on each leg with tone='success' and emphasis=true on price.
Use tone='warning' on stops cell for 1 stop, tone='danger' for 2+ stops.
guardrails: Don't include itineraries with unrealistic layover times.
Don't invent airline logos — use the airline name as the query.
Always show local times with airport code, never UTC.

patchPrefer: Use set_active_tab when the user switches legs
Use remove_items to drop itineraries the user rejected
replaceTriggers: User asks for one-way instead of round trip
